meaning: n.旁轨,侧线,次要地位vt.导入侧线,转移目标,使受牵制
examples: They have a tendency to try to sidetrack you from your task.
The Sidetrack 25 is built for all your urban travel destinations.
When severe, these problems can force to sidetrack or abandon a well.
The company revealed it could return to Alpha at some point in the future to deepen it or drill a sidetrack well.
He insists that in the push to drill the well quickly, the drillers lost circulation and got stuck, and had to regroup and drill a sidetrack well.
